title: Mission Control — React Web UI, WebSocket Fixes, Terminal Sessions

test_results.failed_reason: All pre-existing: test_youtube_live (timeout), test_arxiv_live (timeout), test_arxiv_search_live (timeout), test_daemon_status_when_not_running (daemon is running). No regressions.

overview: Built 'Octane Mission Control' — a full-stack React + FastAPI web dashboard with live WebSocket events, xterm.js PTY terminal, globe visualization, and dark 'Mr. Robot' aesthetic. Fixed critical server crash on browser refresh (os.fork in async loop), rewrote WebSocket system with session pool for terminal persistence and subscriber deduplication, added multi-terminal sessions, port auto-kill, and real-time Synapse event streaming from CLI commands.

parts_completed.Phase1_Backend: FastAPI application at port 44480 — 8 route modules (system, dashboard, models, traces, research, queries, auth, WebSocket), CORS, lifespan context manager, static file serving for React build
parts_completed.Phase2_Frontend: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite 8 app with dark theme (SF Mono, scan-line overlay, #0a0a0f bg, #00ff88 accent). Components: SystemVitals, ModelPanel, EventStream, QueryTimeline, Globe, Header, Panel, Gauge. Pages: Dashboard (3-col hero + 2-col bottom grid), TerminalPage
parts_completed.Phase3_Terminal: xterm.js PTY bridge over WebSocket using subprocess.Popen (not os.fork). Session-based architecture with persistent PTY processes, scrollback buffer (64KB), reconnect with replay, multi-session tab bar
parts_completed.Phase4_WS_Fixes: Single shared EventsContext provider eliminates subscriber leak (was 29+ connections → now 1). Catches RuntimeError alongside WebSocketDisconnect. Terminal sessions survive page navigation (CSS show/hide, not route unmount)
parts_completed.Phase5_Events: Trace file watcher now detects NEW .jsonl files created by octane ask (reads from byte 0 instead of skipping). Events stream to Dashboard Live Events panel and Globe
parts_completed.Phase6_CLI: octane ui start/stop/status. Auto-detect and kill stale PIDs on occupied port via lsof. Port auto-kill with warning message
parts_completed.Phase7_CheatSheet: Created OCTANE_CLI_CheatSheet.md — comprehensive 72-command reference with chaining, pipelining, Shadows, and .octane script file documentation. Deleted outdated OCTANE_CLI.md and Octane_Commands.txt

bugs_fixed: os.fork() inside async uvicorn corrupted event loop → subprocess.Popen
PTY sending bytes to xterm.js → send_text() with UTF-8 decode
Rolldown (Vite 8) stripping type-only exports → export interface directly
WebSocket subscriber leak: 3 components × 2 (StrictMode) = 6+ connections per page load → single EventsContext provider
Terminal RuntimeError: Cannot call receive after disconnect → catch (WebSocketDisconnect, RuntimeError)
Terminal session lost on navigation → session pool with persistent PTY, CSS show/hide instead of route unmount
Trace file watcher skipping new files (seen_sizes defaulting to current_size) → default to 0 for new files
Port 44480 Address already in use → auto-kill via lsof in octane ui start

architecture_notes.terminal_session_pool: TerminalSession dataclass with proc, master_fd, scrollback (64KB), persistent reader_task, session_id UUID. Sessions survive WS disconnect for reconnect. 30min TTL.
architecture_notes.events_architecture: Single EventsProvider React context wraps app → 1 WS connection. All components call useEvents(). Eliminates subscriber leak from per-component connections.
architecture_notes.terminal_persistence: Module-level sessionStore Map survives React re-renders. App uses CSS display:flex/none instead of route mount/unmount. Terminal component never unmounts.

key_decisions: subprocess.Popen over os.fork — fork inside async event loop corrupts it
Session pool over per-WS terminals — enable reconnect and persistence
React Context over per-component WebSocket — single connection prevents subscriber leak
CSS show/hide over Route mount/unmount — keep terminal alive across navigation
Module-level sessionStore over React state — survives component lifecycle
Scrollback replay on reconnect — 64KB buffer per session
lsof-based port auto-kill — prevent Address already in use errors
